label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 11

extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won a windswept sprint after Bike Exchange controlled the finale despite losing Simon Yates to COVID that morning
tactical_patterns: Bike Exchange pivoted from GC support to sprint train within hours of losing Yates, revealing pre-existing sprint contingency planning.
PEDERSEN secured intermediate sprint points despite no threat from Bennett absent, suggesting green jersey points optimization over energy conservation.
UAU chose MILANO over ACKERMANN for leadout duties, indicating internal sprint hierarchy shift or Ackermann form concerns.
Only 3-man breakaway formed despite no mountains points, showing peloton's reluctance to burn matches into headwind on flat stages.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: outsprinted Merlier and Van Poppel with full leadout, confirming升级 to protected sprinter status
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: not selected as UAE's sprint option despite Merlier preference, signals possible form loss or team confidence issue
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: 5th place maintains green jersey dominance but suggests prioritizing points security over stage hunting
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: COVID positive exit eliminates Bike Exchange GC threat, drastically simplifying Evenepoel's path to Madrid
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: shoulder injury crash removes key Evenepoel mountain support with hardest stages approaching
